title: rhinanthus minor subsp. monticola
intro: Rhinanthus minor subsp. monticola, also known as the mountain bee orchid, is a subspecies of the common broomrape plant, which is a hemiparasitic plant that obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rhinanthus minor subsp. monticola include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
